patch.value: Resolve validate duplicate issue-code GH-209 warning without changelog drag

pm validate metadata warns validate_metadata_duplicate_issue_codes for GH-209 shared by closed items pm-1byt (exit-13 top-level-await fix) and pm-25se (OTLP export follow-up). Both live in shipped release buckets; renaming either title now would move it into [Unreleased] because pm-changelog buckets by updated_at (known trap). Resolve by either (a) teaching issue-code dedupe to exempt explicit 'follow-up' titled items so distinct logical work sharing a GH code is not flagged, or (b) performing the rename in a dedicated tracker-only migration where the changelog is regenerated and reviewed in the same commit.

pm validate reports duplicate_issue_codes_count: 0 (or the GH-209 pair is exempted by an agreed follow-up rule with a spec); changelog:pm:check stays green in the same change; no closed item silently migrates release buckets.

## Context
`pm validate` metadata check warns `validate_metadata_duplicate_issue_codes: 1` — issue code GH-209 is shared by two CLOSED items:
- pm-1byt "GH-209: successful pm mutations can exit 13 from unsettled top-level await" (closed 2026-06-12)
- pm-25se "GH-209 follow-up: OTEL span export keeps the CLI alive ~10s (and can exit 13) when the traces endpoint is unreachable" (closed 2026-06-14)

Issue-code extraction is title-prefix based, so the "GH-209 follow-up:" prefix collides with the original. Both items are legitimate distinct work; this is arguably a validator false-positive rather than a data error.

## Why this can't be fixed by a simple rename
pm-changelog buckets closed items by `updated_at`. Touching either closed item now would drag it out of its shipped release section (2026.6.x) into `[Unreleased]`, corrupting the historical changelog and failing `changelog:pm:check` review expectations. This is the known changelog-bucket trap that has bitten twice before.

## Options (pick one)
1. **Validator exemption (preferred):** teach the issue-code dedupe to treat an explicit "follow-up" marker (e.g. title starting `GH-NNN follow-up:`) as a distinct logical code, with a spec pinning the exemption. Fixes the class, touches no closed items, zero changelog risk.
2. **Tracker-only rename migration:** rename pm-25se's title prefix (e.g. `GH-209-FU:`) in a dedicated tracker-only commit where the changelog is regenerated and the bucket move is reviewed/accepted explicitly as part of the same change.

## Evidence
- `pm validate` remediation_hint: "Items pm-1byt, pm-25se share issue code \"GH-209\"; rename or merge so each logical code maps to one item."
- Remediation-registry entry for this warning class already exists (closed pm-sdbo).

author: maintainer-agent
message: Re-parent under new Governance/validation capability epic pm-33cw (was hanging directly off milestone pm-doxj)

author: maintainer-agent
message: Both GH-209 items (pm-1byt, pm-25se) are closed; retitle/merge drags them into [Unreleased] until closed_at is stamped (pm-m4iu) and backfilled (pm-wrbe)
